# Project Guidelines

## Code Style
- Use TypeScript and preserve existing formatting conventions: single quotes, no semicolons, trailing commas.
- Follow ESLint flat config in `frontend/eslint.config.js` and package-local lint scripts.
- Avoid editing generated/build outputs (`dist/**`) and generated router files.

## Architecture
- Monorepo uses pnpm workspaces (`app`, `packages/*`) and Turbo task orchestration.
- `app` is a Vite + React + TanStack Router shell for game routes.
- Game implementations live in package libraries and are consumed through workspace package exports:
  - `@parkerstovall.com/mustachio` exposes `startMustachio`.
  - `@parkerstovall.com/pac-man` exposes `PacMan` React component.
  - `@parkerstovall.com/pac-man` depends on `@parkerstovall.com/pac-man-map-generator`.

## Build and Test
- Install dependencies: `cd frontend && pnpm install`
- Root commands:
  - `pnpm build`
  - `pnpm lint`
  - `pnpm dev` (filtered to app by root script)
- Use package filters for scoped work: `pnpm --filter <package-name> <script>`
- Tests are currently placeholders at root/app; verify scripts before claiming test coverage.

## Project Conventions
- Treat `app/src/routeTree.gen.ts` as generated by TanStack Router; do not hand-edit.
- Prefer exact package names in filter commands:
  - `@parkerstovall.com/app`
  - `@parkerstovall.com/mustachio`
  - `@parkerstovall.com/pac-man`
  - `@parkerstovall.com/pac-man-map-generator`
- Trust `package.json` scripts over README/CLAUDE docs if they disagree.

## Integration Points
- App routes in `app/src/routes/games/*` directly integrate package exports.
- Phaser is shared across game packages and externalized in library builds.
- `pac-man-map-generator` validates options with Zod before generating map data.

## Security
- This workspace is client-side focused; no auth/backend secret patterns were found in these packages.
- Keep all user-provided input validation in map-generation paths intact (`packages/pac-man-map-generator/src/options.ts`).
